data.title: Aave has suspended rsETH reserve operations on Ethereum, Arbitrum, and other networks to reduce systemic risk.
data.description: Aave has released an update on the rsETH security incident on its X platform, disclosing that it has suspended rsETH reserve operations on the Ethereum mainnet and networks such as Arbitrum, Base, Mantle, and Linea. This measure aims to preserve as much capital as possible and reduce systemic risk during the asset recovery process. Aave stated that it will continue to disclose further developments and solutions to the community.

data.title: Aave Suspends rsETH Reserves Across Multiple Networks for Fund Recovery
data.description: On April 23, Aave announced an update regarding the rsETH event. According to BlockBeats, the reserves for rsETH have been suspended on the Ethereum mainnet, Arbitrum, Base, Mantle, and Linea networks. This action is part of a fund recovery plan aimed at reclaiming as much capital as possible. Aave has committed to providing ongoing updates to the community as the situation progresses.

data.title: Barclays Turns Bullish on Amazon Amid Strong AWS AI Revenue
data.description: Barclays has shifted to a bullish stance on Amazon, designating it as its top mega-cap pick following Amazon's announcement that AWS AI revenue has reached a $15 billion annualized run rate in the first quarter of 2026. According to NS3.AI, Barclays also highlighted Amazon's $20 billion in annual revenue from its Trainium and Graviton chips. The article noted a mean price target for Amazon (AMZN) at $286.66, compared to its current price of approximately $248.

data.title: Justin Sun: Current AI Is "Shackled" — Blockchain-Enabled Financial Freedom Will Unlock Its Full Potential
data.description: Tron founder Justin Sun expressed strong long-term conviction in blockchain at the Inside the Blockchain 100 event at Binance Square, arguing that blockchain is the only viable economic infrastructure for the AI era. According to Tron founder Justin Sun at Inside the Blockchain 100, Sun said AI agents are currently in an early and constrained stage, with humans providing them minimal financial empowerment, but predicted that AI will eventually be "liberated" — defined as AI freely conducting blockchain transactions autonomously. He said that milestone will trigger geometric growth in on-chain activity, as a single person directing millions of AI agents could generate transaction volumes far exceeding today's human-driven demand.

data.title: Poly Group Denies Involvement in Commodity Trading Platforms
data.description: Poly Group has clarified that it has not set up any commodity trading platforms or investment websites related to gold, crude oil, or similar assets. According to NS3.AI, the company stated that its name and the names of its subsidiaries have been misused by scammers in fraudulent trading applications, misleading advertisements, and an unrelated construction project associated with the Ningdongta Coal Mine Tailings Management Project.

data.title: Today, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net inflow of 4,349 BTC, and Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 35,736 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 4,349 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 35,736 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 1,311 SOL.

data.title: AI Agents Transition to Execution, Highlighting Need for Crypto Infrastructure
data.description: Vincent Chok of First Digital has highlighted the evolving role of AI agents, which are increasingly moving from providing advice to executing tasks. According to NS3.AI, this shift underscores the potential need for cryptocurrency infrastructure to support these operations. A recent PwC survey involving over 300 companies revealed that 79% are already utilizing AI agents in some capacity. Chok emphasized that stablecoins, blockchains, and crypto wallets are essential for enabling continuous machine payments. However, he noted that security, liability, and regulatory clarity remain significant challenges to broader adoption.

data.title: Trump's Iran Policy Sparks Surge in Prediction Market Activity
data.description:
On April 23, prediction market platforms experienced a significant increase in trading volume amid uncertainty surrounding U.S. President Donald Trump's policy on the Iran conflict, according to BlockBeats. Data from Dune indicates that between April 5 and April 8, the day after Trump announced a ceasefire, Polymarket recorded 413 million trades related to the Iran war, with transactions exceeding $100 million.

The report highlights that on April 8, Polymarket saw nearly 100,000 trades concerning the possibility of Trump deploying troops to Iran, marking the highest single-day trading volume of the year at that time. Following Trump's April 5 post on Truth Social demanding Iran to "open the damn strait," trading on whether an invasion was imminent surged. On April 7, after Trump hinted at "the demise of an entire civilization tonight," the topic shifted to whether a ceasefire would occur, becoming the most traded event.

Dune's report describes Trump as an "unpredictable machine," noting how his social media-driven governance style has fueled trading activity. Prediction markets have faced bipartisan criticism for allegedly promoting insider trading, yet Trump appears supportive, adopting a relaxed regulatory approach and aiding the industry's expansion. Reports indicate Trump's eldest son, Donald Trump Jr., holds shares in Polymarket and serves as an advisor to Kalshi. Polymarket's valuation has reached $9.6 billion, nearly tenfold growth since the latest investment round by Trump Jr.'s venture capital fund eight months ago. The Trump Organization is reportedly developing its own prediction market platform, Truth Predict.

Andrew Surabian, a spokesperson for Trump Jr., responded to related concerns, stating, "Don's involvement as an investor or advisor in any company does not engage with the federal government and has no influence or participation in government policy regarding prediction markets." Polymarket has not commented on media inquiries. Additionally, the probability of Trump facing impeachment before the end of his term on Polymarket has risen from 13% at the beginning of the year to 66% as of Tuesday.

data.title: Polymarket Faces Insider Trading Allegations Over Trump-Related Predictions
data.description:
Polymarket, a cryptocurrency prediction market platform, is embroiled in controversy over insider trading allegations linked to predictions about U.S. President Donald Trump's policies and statements. According to Odaily, data reveals that from April 5 to 8, markets related to the Iran situation generated approximately 413 million predictions, involving over $100 million.

Analysts note that Trump's unpredictable decision-making style has significantly increased activity in prediction markets. Topics such as potential military action against Iran and ceasefire initiatives have become frequent trading subjects, with transaction volumes surging following his social media posts.

Notably, Donald Trump Jr. reportedly holds shares in Polymarket and serves as an advisor to another prediction platform, Kalshi, raising concerns about potential conflicts of interest and insider trading. Industry data indicates that political predictions have become the second-largest category in prediction markets, following sports. Despite the growing controversy, U.S. regulators maintain a generally lenient stance, allowing the sector to continue expanding.

data.title: Bitwarden CLI Hijacked: Malicious Package Targets Crypto Wallets and Developer Credentials
data.description: Attackers have compromised Bitwarden's CLI version 2026.4.0 by releasing a malicious npm package designed to steal cryptocurrency wallet data and developer credentials. According to NS3.AI, the breach was identified by Socket on April 23 and is associated with the TeamPCP supply chain campaign. The affected workflows could have exposed sensitive information, including GitHub and npm tokens, SSH keys, cloud credentials, and exchange API credentials used in automated cryptocurrency deployments.

data.title: Iran Denies Charging Hormuz Strait Fees in Cryptocurrency
data.description: Iranian media outlet Fars News has reported that claims regarding Iran charging passage fees for the Hormuz Strait in cryptocurrency are inaccurate. According to Odaily, the reports circulating about this matter have been clarified by Iranian sources, dismissing the notion that cryptocurrency is being used for such transactions.

data.title: Iranian Forces Display Strength in Hormuz Strait
data.description: Iran's judiciary chief, Ejei, expressed pride in the display of power by Iranian armed forces in the Hormuz Strait on social media. According to Odaily, Ejei stated that three more vessels violating regulations were dealt with in the strategic waterway on the 22nd. He remarked that 'Americans dare not approach the Hormuz Strait,' referencing the fate of the two so-called advanced destroyers, 'USS Michael Murphy' and 'USS Peterson.' Ejei also noted that the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps has deployed a 'swarm' fleet of speedboats and drones around the caves near Forur Island, maintaining vigilance against U.S. naval vessels and attempting to suppress them through 'saturation' tactics.

data.title: Peter Schiff Criticizes Strategy's STRC Preferred Stock
data.description: Peter Schiff has expressed concerns about Strategy's STRC preferred stock, likening it to a Ponzi scheme. According to NS3.AI, Schiff argues that the company's software revenue is insufficient to meet the 11.5% annual dividend. He emphasized that the dividend is optional and not a legal requirement, cautioning that STRC holders might face a significant price decline if demand decreases, as they cannot redeem their shares.

data.title: Iran's Chief Justice stated that the US dares not approach the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Iranian Justice Chief Justice Ejai posted on social media that the display of strength by the Iranian armed forces in the Strait of Hormuz was "a source of pride." He stated that on the 22nd, three more vessels violating regulations were taken into custody in this strategic waterway. Ejai claimed that "the Americans dare not approach the Strait of Hormuz," and added that "they have already seen the fate of those two so-called super-advanced destroyers, the USS Michael Murphy and the USS Peterson." He also stated that a "swarm" fleet of speedboats and drones from the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps is deployed from caves around Frolul Island, maintaining vigilance against US warships and attempting to strike them through "saturation" suppression tactics. (CCTV News)

data.title: South African Wheat Planting Declines Amid Rising Costs
data.description: South African farmers are expected to plant the smallest area of wheat in over a decade this year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the ongoing conflict in Iran has led to increased costs for essential inputs like fertilizer and fuel. These rising expenses are impacting agricultural decisions, prompting farmers to reduce wheat cultivation. The situation underscores the broader economic challenges faced by the agricultural sector in South Africa, as geopolitical tensions continue to affect global supply chains and commodity prices.

data.title: Morpho Association Plans Series of Wallet Transfers for Fund Management
data.description: Morpho has announced plans to conduct a series of transfers from its Association-owned wallets in the coming weeks as part of its fund management strategy. According to Foresight News, the organization aims to optimize its financial operations through these transactions. The details of the transfers have not been disclosed, but they are expected to enhance the efficiency of Morpho's asset management processes.

data.title: DeFi Security Concerns Limit Institutional Interest, Says JPMorgan
data.description: JPMorgan has highlighted ongoing security vulnerabilities in decentralized finance (DeFi) as a significant barrier to institutional adoption. According to NS3.AI, the bank pointed to the recent KelpDAO exploit, which resulted in a loss of approximately $20 billion in total value locked (TVL) within a few days. The attacker reportedly minted $292 million in unbacked rsETH, leaving around $200 million in bad debt across various lending protocols. Following the exploit, capital shifted from DeFi lending platforms to Tether's USDT, while ether-denominated TVL remained largely stable.

data.title: Galaxy Research Director: Strategy's Bitcoin Holdings May Surpass Satoshi Nakamoto's Within Two Years
data.description: Alex Thorn, Head of Research at Galaxy, stated in an article published on the X platform that Strategy currently holds more Bitcoin than the world's largest Bitcoin ETF, IBIT. Based on the current trend of increasing holdings, Strategy is expected to surpass Satoshi Nakamoto within the next two years to become one of the world's largest Bitcoin holders.

data.title: U.S. EIA Natural Gas Inventory Exceeds Expectations
data.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) reported that natural gas inventories for the week ending April 17 reached 103 billion cubic feet. According to Jin10, this figure surpassed the expected 94 billion cubic feet and was significantly higher than the previous week's 59 billion cubic feet.

data.title: Tether Mints 350 Million USDT Amid Market Activity
data.description: According to NS3.AI, Whale Alert has reported the minting of 350 million USDT by Tether. This development comes as the cryptocurrency market experiences fluctuations, with investors closely monitoring the impact of such large-scale minting on market liquidity and stability. The creation of new USDT tokens often signals increased demand or strategic positioning by major players in the market. Analysts are observing how this influx might influence trading volumes and price movements across various digital assets.
